title: Roller Rink
shortDescription: A looping 90s-themed roller rink with arcade, food court and skate rentals; a friendly native, the Coach, offers lessons and guidance.
atmosphere: Retro rink vibes: pop music, laughter and food-court smells over nostalgic neon carpeting — inviting and non-threatening.
tips: Talk to the Coach for lessons and directions. Food-court staff give free meals if you don't mention the Backrooms. The arcade no-clips to Level 40.

title: Forest of the Skeleton Queen
shortDescription: An endless dark forest of hanging skeletons under a moonless sky, ruled by a skeletal queen who may grant wishes for the price of a soul.
atmosphere: Perpetual night lit only by moonlight on bone and bark; haunting winds whisper through the remains — oppressive and sorrowful.
tips: Show the Skeleton Queen respect; she may bargain rather than kill. Never chase The Belle unless ready for fatal consequences.

title: The Infinite Mall
shortDescription: An infinite shopping mall that decays the further you travel, from clean stores into flooded, hazardous ruins.
atmosphere: Clean and quiet at first, turning flooded, moldy and overgrown as mental deterioration and hallucinations creep in.
tips: Try not to travel beyond ~50 miles. Stay hydrated, manage the psychological effects and watch for entity activity that grows with distance.

title: Sewer System
shortDescription: A tight, pitch-black sewer of brick and stone where the water below may drop you into other levels. Home to several dangerous entities.
atmosphere: Claustrophobic darkness over stagnant water, hostile creatures lurking in the cramped tunnels.
tips: Bring a light and almond water. Kill your flashlight if you meet Smilers. Never fall into the water.

title: An Empty Car Park
shortDescription: A six-floor concrete parking garage lit in colored light, ringed by a dark abyss and holding anomalous vehicles with unpredictable effects.
atmosphere: Dim warm and colored lighting over a mostly empty, eerie garage, with dangerous cars and reality-bending exits.
tips: Don't approach the edges or the cars flood in. Use vehicles strategically to reach other levels, or cross the cars to safe staircases.

title: Timeless Airport
shortDescription: A vast non-Euclidean airport of 26 terminals with flights to nearly every level; severe time dilation makes every wait feel eternal.
atmosphere: Sterile timeless terminals under fluorescent light: endless corridors, shops and gate lounges warped by disorienting geometry.
tips: Follow signage carefully through the non-Euclidean areas; empty signs mean dead ends. Ask the Backrooms Travel Agency at check-in for routes.

title: Sublimity
shortDescription: A vast submerged complex of white ceramic rooms flooded with relaxing salt water, its surreal architecture serving no clear purpose.
atmosphere: Serene yet unsettling calm over crystalline pools under perfect light; strange geometry and altered acoustics undercut the peace.
tips: Appears entirely entity-free and restful. Avoid the deep pits and dark tunnels; the Epsom-salt water relaxes but long immersion is untested.

title: Fold Point
shortDescription: A convergence point where Levels 0–37 physically overlap, fusing the architecture and hazards of every earlier level.
atmosphere: A chaotic, disorienting amalgam of clashing level designs, badly distorted and dark, in constant structural flux.
tips: Photos barely work here. Head toward stabilizing geometry to exit. Don't no-clip outward or you risk the Blue Channel.

title: Enchanted Forest
shortDescription: A finite circular forest of twisting dirt paths locked in twilight, inducing nostalgia and a hypnotic calm that tempts dangerously long stays.
atmosphere: Perpetual dusk over sparse ancient oaks, cool and unnaturally tranquil yet disorienting, pulling the mind toward inaction.
tips: Drink Almond Water to break the hypnotic effect. Don't linger near the center. Navigate by the paths and beware the Border's harsh weather.

title: Roller Rockin' Pizza!
shortDescription: A retro 1980s arcade of playable machines, vending sauce best left alone, and unseen entities that quietly use the games.
atmosphere: Neon-lit arcade humming with machines and chatter, thick with the smell of decades-old pizza and stale soda.
tips: Don't play the anomalous games — especially 'General Starflare: Wild Space'. Never drink the glowing Universal Sauce. Pat at the desk is safe.
